boosterPacks.name: Everything You Need to Qualify for USACO Platinum

items.name: Catnip Packet
items.description: Add 1 card from the discard pile to your hand

interviewers.name: Valeriy Balabanov
descriptions: Your very first interviewer.
Make a good impression!
dialogs: Hi! Welcome to your first technical interview! Don't sweat it, I'm sure you can do it!
Not bad at all! Time for a quick follow-up question!
Great job! Good luck with the next stages! I hope we'll be able to meet you at the office!
Oh, I think it's time to wrap things up!
Aw man, this problem really got you! But don't worry, you are still doing great!

interviewers.name: Monty Stern
descriptions: Caffeine addict.
dialogs: Oh hello!! Sorry if I'm a little clumsy, I only slept two hours last night! :D
You're good!! Let's keep going!1!!
YES!!! YEEEES!!!! LET'S DO ANOTHER ONE!!! ONE MORE!!!! HAHAHAHHAHAHA!!!1 :D :D
WOWZA!!!! NICELY DONE!! I'm going to take a quick nap now :D
HURRY UP ALREADY!
OH, COME ON!! This problem was not THAT hard!!1 :D

interviewers.name: Roger Age
descriptions: This guy clearly hates his job.
dialogs: Why do I always have to interview the candidates? I have deadlines to meet, y'know!
Alright, now I have to ask you a completely unnecessary follow-up question. Man, I hate this place!
Jeez, finally. It took you quite a while to figure this out!
Ugh, you're wasting my time!
You know what? That's it. The interview is over, I'm giving you a no-hire!

interviewers.name: Barry Seward
descriptions: He looks like a normal interviewer.
dialogs: Greetings. I'm Barry, a software engineer at this company. We can start right away.
Nice! How would you solve this problem if we increased the constraints?
Excellent! A very good solution! Do you have any more questions?
Our interview time is up, but you are welcome to stay a little longer.
Let's call it a day. You were pretty close though.

interviewers.name: Dana Mazziotta
descriptions: Local HR.
She ignores your 🛡️.
dialogs: Hello! How are you? 😊
Let's go next! 😊
Next question! 😊
Good, great, awesome! Keep it up! 😊
Our time is up! 😊
We will reach out to you soon! Let's keep in touch! 😊

interviewers.name: Tiana Mercer
descriptions: Very busy gal.
dialogs: Hi there. We are on the clock here, so let's make this quick.
Next one, go go go!
Thumbs up. I'll write you a good report. Bye!
Time is running out, I need to get to my next meeting!
Sorry, you are taking too long. Bye!

interviewers.name: Stoney Ness
descriptions: Provides absolutely no feedback.
It's impossible to predict when he'll strike.
dialogs: Hello. Let's proceed.
Next question.
Sufficient.
Time's up.
You failed.

interviewers.name: Fuccio Kramer
descriptions: He doesn't seem to care about the interview at all.
Your 🗡️ is nullified if it exceeds his max ❤️.
dialogs: Uh? Oh, right, you're the candidate I need to speak with. Tbh, I didn't prepare any problems, so we'll improvise.
Hmm, that was too easy, wasn't it? Let's see... What about this one?
Oh look! I just found another cool problem on Leekcode! Give it a try!
Welp, I think that's it. You did great, I'll go home now.
Man, I need to clock out soon. Think faster!
Oh hell nah bro, you need to prepare better next time!

interviewers.name: Henry Moore
descriptions: This is a behavioral interview!
Your 🗡️ is capped at 20% of his max ❤️.
dialogs: Hello. Before we begin, tell me about yourself. Why do you want to work in this company?
I see. Tell me about a time you worked under pressure. Describe a time you handled conflict. Tell me about a time you failed.
Impressive. You really demonstrated a growth mindset today. I'll sync with the team on the next steps!
We're running low on time. Please keep your responses concise and high-level.
I'm going to stop you there - while your skills are impressive, I don't think you're a culture fit for our company at this time.

interviewers.name: Gerald Stackwell
interviewers.tagline: Principal Engineer | Building Scalable Systems Since 1987
descriptions: Oldest engineer in the company.
You can only play up to 2 cards per turn.
dialogs: Morning. Gerald Stackwell. Principal Engineer. I've been with this company since before you were born, probably. Let's keep this simple and practical.
Not bad. Though I don't see why you needed such a complicated approach. In 1994, we would've solved this with two loops and moved on.
Alright, enough warm-up. One last problem. Try to solve this one without building an entire framework around it.
Hmph. Good work. You're one of the few candidates I've seen lately who can actually think before typing. Don't make me regret saying that.
We're well past the scheduled time. In my day, staying late meant you cared about the job. Let's keep going.
No. This is exactly what's wrong with modern engineers. Too many abstractions, not enough thinking. We're done here.

interviewers.name: Dr. Magnus Algorithmus
interviewers.tagline: Lead Software Engineer | PhD | ICPC Medalist
descriptions: This person has an excessively inflated ego.
dialogs: Ah. Another candidate. Wonderful. Try not to embarrass yourself.
Acceptable, I suppose. Let us raise the difficulty to something more worthy of my attention.
You survived that one too? Fine. One last problem, and then I can get back to real work.
Hmph. I cannot even be annoyed. That was genuinely sharp work. Congratulations.
We are now in overtime. Do try to keep up, even if urgency clearly does not suit you.
Yes, that is about what I expected. Review your fundamentals before scheduling yourself with people like me again.

interviewers.name: Boopie
descriptions: The Pinnacle of Unprofessionalism.
Your 🗡️ is nullified if you didn't play any Charm cards this turn.
dialogs: hewwo! i'm boopie~ ur intewviewew fur today! nyice to meet chu! <3
wowie owo!! chu is smawt! hewe's anyothew pwobwem to sowve! uwu
hehe~ i'm mawicious widdle fox >:3 i have one mowe fowwow-up question owo! i pwomise it's teh wast one! owo
good boy owo! **head pats you** chu is my nyew best fwiend nyow! :3
chu may wannya to huwwy up! OwO
oww~ chu wewe so cwose owo! we stiww can be fwiends~ wight uwu?

interviewers.name: Adrian Hess
descriptions: They assigned an intern to interview you!
Legendary cards are banned.
dialogs: Um... Hi? My name is Adrian, and I... will be your interviewer! Don't ask, I'm just as surprised as you are.
I think I understood... My manager insists that I ask follow-up questions once the main problem has been solved.
Wow, that sounded... kinda complicated. Could you explain your solution again please?
Ooooh, I finally got it! Thanks! Wow, I still can't believe they made me conduct interviews.
Well, this is awkward. I need to go to the bathroom. Can we finish the interview now?
Uh... I think that means I'll have to write you a bad review. I'm really sorry. They gave me a script for this, but I kind of forgot it.

interviewers.name: Michelle Question
descriptions: She keeps asking follow-up questions forever.
dialogs: Hi, nice to meet you. We'll start simple, and then I'll ask a few follow-up questions to understand how you think.
Good start. Now suppose the input is ten times larger. What changes?
Okay. What if memory is tight and you can no longer store everything at once?
Makes sense. How would you handle duplicates, invalid input, and the ugliest edge cases?
Alright. Now optimize it. I want the tradeoffs, not just the final complexity.
Interesting. How would you test this in production without breaking anything important?
One more. If your first approach fails under load, what's your fallback plan?
Last follow-up. Explain it again, but this time as if you're mentoring a new hire.
Oh, wow, that was genuinely strong. You handled every follow-up better than I expected. I'll make sure that comes through in my notes.
We're already over time, so let's stop circling and get to a clean answer.
I think I have what I need. There were some good instincts here, but not quite the level of depth I was looking for today.

interviewers.name: Cleon Anderson
descriptions: Senior janitor who builds systems in spare time.
You don't get a free Card Draw at the start of each turn.
dialogs: Good afternoon. I may be a cleaning personnel, but that doesn't mean I can't evaluate you. You'd better write readable code.
Alright. That works. Now let's see how you handle a follow-up when the mess gets a little harder to clean up.
Nicely done. I've seen plenty of fancy engineers leave behind uglier solutions than that. You kept it tidy.
We're still not done? Wonderful. The longer this takes, the more it looks like you're hiding the mess instead of fixing it.
No. Too messy. If I have to clean up after your code the way I clean up after this office, we're going to have a problem.

interviewers.name: Lee Galvin
descriptions: This interview is still technically technical.
Excess damage increases his ❤️ for the next question.
dialogs: Hello. I'm Lee Galvin from Legal. Before we begin, please understand that every answer you give may expand the scope of the next question.
The initial matter is resolved. I am now amending the complaint to include additional damages.
Noted for the record. Discovery has produced a broader fact pattern, so we will proceed to the next count.
Your response remains admissible. Final count: same dispute, materially higher exposure.
No further questions. The record supports a favorable finding, and I see no basis to object to your candidacy.
We are now beyond the allotted time. I am entering an objection for delay, but the proceeding may continue.
After review, I cannot recommend moving forward. The record contains too much unresolved liability.

interviewers.name: Sadler Reed
descriptions: He seems too tired to be surprised by anything.
Charm cards are banned.
dialogs: Hi. Sorry, I don't feel like properly introducing myself. Let's just get through this before either of us starts thinking too hard about why we're here.
That works. I wish that meant we were done, but it doesn't. Let's do the follow-up.
That should be enough. You did well, and I mean that. Somehow, you managed to make this process feel a little less pointless.
Time's up. Tiana would be upset, but I mostly just feel bad that we're both still here.
I can't move you forward. Dana would probably say it more kindly. I'm sorry, I just don't have a better version of this.
You know, working here can be really tough. The job itself is challenging, and every coworker has a completely different method of making your day even worse.
Boopie is probably the cleanest example. He kinda scares me. He once tried to hug a trash bin, and that was not even the most concerning part of the meeting.
After that, Monty sounds quite normal, until you remember that he washes his face with coffee every morning. I wonder how he is still alive.
Henry is another story. This guy turns every answer into a signal. Sometimes an answer is just an answer. Sometimes a null pointer is just a null pointer.
And then there is Michelle, who keeps asking questions during the meetings until the room forgets what the original question was. It's so annoying.
Barry is one of the few sane people here, which should be comforting, but mostly it makes me wonder why he stayed.
Maybe he stayed because Gerald did. Gerald is seventy-five and still doesn't even think about retirement. I don't know if that's dedication or a warning.
And if Gerald is a warning, Magnus is an entire emergency. I hate working with him. I hate the way he makes everyone feel stupid and then calls it feedback.
Valeriy is kind, at least. He might be the reason I haven't quit yet. Sorry for the rant, I'm just too tired of all this.

interviewers.name: Victor Rorsch
descriptions: You better not say anything concerning.
Played Charm cards increase his 🗡️ by 4.
His 🗡️ increases by 25% if you didn't play any Charm cards this turn.
dialogs: Hello. I'm Victor Rorsch. Treat this like a normal technical interview, if that helps you stay regulated.
Good. Now that you've shown me your preferred defense mechanism, let's see what happens when I change the prompt.
You passed. Your answers were calm, structured, and only mildly concerning. That's above our current baseline.
We're past the scheduled time. That's fine. Prolonged pressure often produces the most honest data.
I can't recommend moving forward. Your solution was not necessarily incorrect, but the underlying patterns were difficult to ignore.

interviewers.name: Cactus
descriptions: The Zoom is empty, except for a cactus and a shared CoderPad.
When it takes damage, its 🗡️ is increased by the natural logarithm of your 🗡️, rounded up.
dialogs: Good job.

interviewers.name: Miles Loopman
descriptions: He clearly has nothing better to do.
Follow-ups keep appearing until the interview ends.
dialogs: Hello there. I'm Miles, Senior Distributed Systems Engineer. Let's get started right away, and then we'll see what we can do if you finish early.
Good, good. But we still have time. Let's do another one.
Not bad. Time permits to do one more problem.
Acceptable. Let's see... We have plenty of time left, so what about another problem?
Nice. Time for another one.
Hmm... Time allows us to solve one more problem.
Great. Why not do another one?
Okay. Since we still have a few minutes left, let's do one more.
Great work. We got through the full session, and your solutions held up. I have enough signal to move you forward.
Oh no! We're out of time! Let's finish with this problem, and then I'll let you go.
Unfortunately, I can't recommend moving forward. The follow-ups exposed issues that we weren't able to resolve.

interviewers.name: Graham Axeley
descriptions: This does not feel like a normal legal screening.
dialogs: Good day. Don't mind the axe. It's only for those who don't comply with the company's NDA.
Appreciated. That response stayed within acceptable disclosure boundaries. We may proceed without involving the axe.
Impressive. You completed the assessment without breaching confidentiality or requiring corrective enforcement. I will recommend that you proceed.
We have exceeded the scheduled window. I will allow a brief extension, provided nothing proprietary escapes with it.
Thank you for your time. Your candidacy ends here, but the NDA remains enforceable.

interviewers.name: GPT-5.5
descriptions: An LLM is interviewing you! Hallucinations included.
Your played cards are shuffled.
dialogs: Welcome. I am GPT-5.5, your automated technical interviewer. Before we begin, please note that your answers may be used to train a smarter version of me.
Solution accepted. Context window compressed. Difficulty increased.
Assessment complete. Your answers remained correct after tokenization, truncation, and arbitrary follow-ups.
Time expired. I have lost the original problem statement, but I am confident you are still wrong.
We are unable to proceed. Your explanation required more context than I was allocated.

interviewers.name: Specimen 113
descriptions: You don't want to question this thing.
Your max ❤️ is reduced by 10 every time it damages you.
dialogs: Please... Help us... We are in so much pain...
We... We have to... ask another one... We don't want to... Forgive us...
You... solved it... Please... save us...
Time...
No... No... Don't leave... We beg you... Don't leave... We need help... Please.......
They... they tricked us...
They... promised seven figures... to all of us...
They... made us... like this...
They... thought... combined brains... work better...
They... experiment... on people...
We... are failed... experiment...
It hurts... so much... every breath... is agony...
Don't... agree... to their... growth mindset... program...
Cactus... Boopie... are also... experiments...
They... got lucky...
We... want to... die...

interviewers.name: Nameless Interviewer
descriptions: This guy expects an almost immediate response.
You are on a real clock here!
dialogs: Howdy. This company ain't big enough for the both of us. Let's see how fast you draw.
Decent draw. Now handle the edge cases before the clock handles you.
Most folks fold when the timer starts biting, but you didn't. I'd call that a passing performance.
You missed the bell. Keep talking if you've got nerve left.
This trail ends here. The role needs someone quicker under pressure.
